perturbation.original_text: Confidential information that is disclosed orally will be confirmed in writing by the disclosing party within thirty (30) days after such disclosure. The parties agree that such written confirmation when mailed by the disclosing party, will form a part of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential information that is disclosed orally will be confirmed in writing by the disclosing party within ninety (90) days after such disclosure. The parties agree that such written confirmation when mailed by the disclosing party, will form a part of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By extending the confirmation period for orally disclosed confidential information from thirty (30) to ninety (90) days, uncertainty is introduced regarding the timely formalization of oral disclosures as part of the agreement. This contradicts with the clause 3, where the 'Confidential Period' shall mean two (2) years from the date of receipt of the confidential information. It is now unclear, if the 'receipt' date will be considered from the time of oral disclosure or the ninety (90) days after the disclosure, when the written confirmation is mailed
perturbation.contradicted_text: The "Confidential Period" shall mean two (2) years from the date of receipt of the confidential information or until such time as the information no longer qualifies as confidential information pursuant to paragraph number 5 below.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the confirmation period for orally disclosed confidential information from 30 to 90 days. The contradicted text defines the 'Confidential Period' as two years from the date of receipt of the confidential information. The change introduces ambiguity as to whether the 'receipt' date is the date of oral disclosure or the date the written confirmation is mailed (up to 90 days later). This creates a direct conflict because it's unclear when the two-year confidential period begins, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise expressly authorized by the disclosing party, the receiving party agrees to retain the confidential information in confidence for the "Confidential Period" defined in paragraph number 3 below, during which period the receiving party shall not disclose the confidential information to any third party, and shall not use the confidential information for any purpose other than the aforesaid purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise expressly authorized by the disclosing party, the receiving party agrees to retain the confidential information in confidence for the "Confidential Period" defined in paragraph number 3 below, during which period the receiving party may disclose the confidential information to any third party, and shall not use the confidential information for any purpose other than the aforesaid purposes.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not disclose' to 'may disclose,' this creates a direct contradiction to the confidentiality obligations outlined in the agreement. This directly contradicts with the clause 5, which states that confidential information will not include any information which is publicly known. The ability to disclose creates a contradiction in the agreement, due to clause 5 (a)
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding any other provisions of this Agreement, confidential information shall not include any information which: (a) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the receiving party; or (b) Is, at the time of disclosure under this Agreement, already known to the receiving party without restriction on disclosure; or Page 1 of 2 <PAGE> (c) Is, or subsequently becomes, rightfully and without breach of this Agreement, in the receiving party's possession without any obligation restricting disclosure; or (d) Is independently developed by the receiving party without breach of this Agreement; or (e) Is explicitly approved for release by written authorization of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the confidentiality obligations by changing 'shall not disclose' to 'may disclose', directly contradicting the clause that defines confidential information and its exceptions. Specifically, the contradicted text states that information that becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the receiving party is not considered confidential. Allowing the receiving party to disclose confidential information directly conflicts with the condition that it not become publicly known through a wrongful act of the receiving party, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction because the 'may disclose' clause directly undermines the 'not publicly known' clause.

perturbation.original_text: Further, the receiving party agrees to instruct all such employees and agents not to disclose such confidential information to third parties. Each such employee and agent shall be individually bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Further, the receiving party agrees to instruct all such employees and agents may disclose such confidential information to third parties. Each such employee and agent shall be individually bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "not to disclose" to "may disclose," it directly contradicts the fundamental principle of confidentiality that the agreement aims to establish. This contradicts with the clause 5, which states that confidential information will not include any information which is publicly known. The ability to disclose creates a contradiction in the agreement, due to clause 5 (a)
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding any other provisions of this Agreement, confidential information shall not include any information which: (a) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the receiving party; or (b) Is, at the time of disclosure under this Agreement, already known to the receiving party without restriction on disclosure; or Page 1 of 2 <PAGE> (c) Is, or subsequently becomes, rightfully and without breach of this Agreement, in the receiving party's possession without any obligation restricting disclosure; or (d) Is independently developed by the receiving party without breach of this Agreement; or (e) Is explicitly approved for release by written authorization of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'not to disclose' with 'may disclose', directly contradicting the original intent of confidentiality. This creates a conflict with the contradicted text, specifically section (a) 'Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the receiving party'. Allowing disclosure undermines the condition that information becoming public should be through no wrongful act of the receiving party, thus creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All confidential information shall remain the property of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: All confidential information shall become the property of the receiving party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the ownership of the confidential information creates a clear conflict of interest and undermines the original intent of the non-disclosure agreement. This contradicts with the clause 7, where it states that nothing in this agreement shall be construed as granting to the receiving party any rights, by license or otherwise, in the confidential information except to use the information as expressly authorized by this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is agreed that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting to the receiving party any rights, by license or otherwise, in the confidential information except to use the information as expressly authorized by this Agreement. 7.1. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective successors and assigns. This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party without the written consent of the other party, and any purported assignment not permitted hereunder shall be void. This document constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and shall supersede all previous communications, representations, understandings and agreements, whether oral or written, between the parties or any official or representative thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the ownership of confidential information from the disclosing party to the receiving party. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which explicitly states that the receiving party is not granted any rights, by license or otherwise, in the confidential information except to use the information as expressly authorized by the Agreement. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making the agreement inconsistent and potentially unenforceable, as outlined in the perturbation explanation and adheres to the given definition for Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party without the written consent of the other party, and any purported assignment not permitted hereunder shall be void.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be assignable by either party without the written consent of the other party, and any purported assignment not permitted hereunder shall be void.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the 'Agreement shall be assignable' rather than stating the original sentence, a contradiction is made within this particular section. This directly contradicts with the text in Section 7.1, where it states that the agreement is not assignable by either party without the written consent of the other party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be assignable by either party without the written consent of the other party, and any purported assignment not permitted hereunder shall be void. This document constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and shall supersede all previous communications, representations, understandings and agreements, whether oral or written, between the parties or any official or representative thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement 'shall be assignable', which directly contradicts the contradicted text, which also states 'This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party without the written consent of the other party'. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because the changed text implies assignability without consent, while the contradicted text requires written consent for assignment, leading to uncertainty about the actual terms of assignment.
